title: Slow-cooker beef and ale stew
subtitle: Dark ale, root vegetables, and braising beef, low and slow
excerpt: Beef chuck braised in dark ale and beef stock with carrots, parsnips, and onion for eight hours until the meat is completely tender. The ale softens to a malt-edged, slightly bitter sauce. No dumplings here — serve with mashed potato or crusty bread.
sourceNotes: Beef cooked in ale is a British tradition predating the use of wine — ale was the more accessible liquid in medieval and post-medieval Britain. The slow cooker adaptation follows the same logic as a Dutch-oven braise: long, low, covered. Dark ales (stout, porter, brown ale) work better than pale ales, which can turn the sauce bitter. Guinness is the most common choice for this style of stew in British home cooking.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better made a day ahead. Skim the solidified fat before reheating.

content.text: Use a dark ale — stout, porter, or brown ale. Pale ales can turn the sauce harsh over a long cook, whereas dark ales mellow to a rounded, malt-forward depth. A 440 ml can of Guinness is the right quantity and the most reliable choice.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over high heat. Season the {{beef-chuck}} and brown in batches until deeply coloured on all sides. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{onion}} to the pan and fry for 3 minutes. Add the {{garlic}} and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the {{guinness}} and scrape up the browned bits. Add the {{stock-beef}} and {{worcestershire-sauce}} and bring to a simmer. Pour over the beef in the slow cooker. Add the {{carrot}}, {{parsnip}}, {{thyme-fresh}}, {{bay-leaves}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Cook on low for 8 hours, until the beef is completely tender. Remove the thyme sprigs and bay leaves. Serve with mashed potato.

items.cause: Used a pale or hoppy ale rather than a dark one
items.fix: Dark ales (stout, porter) mellow in the long cook. Pale ales with high hop bitterness intensify. Stick to Guinness or a dark brown ale for this recipe

items.cause: Lid not sealing or too much liquid added
items.fix: If the sauce is thin at the end, transfer the liquid to a saucepan and reduce over high heat for 10 minutes before returning it to the pot

items.symptom: Vegetables turning to mush by serving time
items.cause: Cut too small or cooking time too long
items.fix: Cut root vegetables into large chunks (4 to 5 cm). They will soften considerably over 8 hours — too small and they disappear

content.text: Beef and ale stew is a pub and home kitchen standard across Britain, particularly in autumn and winter. The slow cooker version makes it a realistic weekday dinner: built in the morning, ready in the evening, the kitchen smelling of something worthwhile when the household returns. It feeds six comfortably and reheats better than it serves fresh.
